reasoning: The threshold requires more than 10 'Battles' (roughly 11+ events) between February 20 and March 21, 2026, within Azerbaijan. According to our evidence, as of March 15, there have been 0 ground battles. The key escalation point was an Iranian drone strike on March 5 in Nakhchivan; however, ACLED codes standalone drone strikes as 'Explosions/Remote violence' rather than 'Battles', which strictly require violent exchange between armed groups on the ground. Though Azerbaijan has mobilized troops to the Iranian border, no infantry clashes have occurred to date. To breach the 11+ threshold, an intense multi-front ground war would need to spontaneously erupt and produce at least 11 discrete 'Battle' events in the remaining 6 days. While tensions are highly elevated, Iran has denied the strike and a full-scale ground invasion is unlikely on such short notice. Thus, the probability of exceeding the threshold is very low.
